rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate, Or In The Alternative, Partially Vacate And Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 12, 2024, Line 1. 1 - RESPONDENT MING LIU's Petition To Vacate, Or In The Alternative, Partially Vacate And Correct Arbitration Award. Respondent Ming Liu's petition to vacate is denied. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. DOE 1, A DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 12, 2024, Line 2. DEFENDANT BIG BROTHERS BIG SISTERS OF AMERICA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Big Brothers Big Sisters of America's ("BBBSA") motion for summary judgment is denied. The court rules as follows on the alternative motions for summary adjudication. The court views the evidence in the light most favorable to plaintiff. (Elk Hills Power, LLC v. Board of Equalization (2013) 57 Cal.4th 593, 606.) BBBSA's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to cause of action one [negligence]. BBBSA fails to maintain its burden of production per CCP 437c(p)(2) because it fails to show that it did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: Y.P. VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 12, 2024, Line 9. DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD FINANCIAL SERVICES GROUP, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The Hartford Financial Services Group, Inc.'s (HFSG) motion to quash supposed service of summons is granted. Plaintiff's return shows purported service on Corporation Trust Company. (Memo. 5:20-24.) But HFSG says (and plaintiff's opposition does not dispute) CT is not HFSG's agent. (Id. at 6:1-3.) If necessary, other issues may be addressed after valid service is made on HFSG. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
